As companies rely on artificial intelligence to increase capital expenditures, banks and private credit institutions compete for customers, and the commercial loan business shows signs of recovery.

From left to right: Bank of America Chairman and CEO Brian Moynihan, JPMorgan Chase Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon, and Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser attended the Senate Banking Committee hearing and testified.

The market generally expects that when major banks, led by JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America, release their second-quarter earnings reports on Tuesday, revenue from stock and fixed-income trading businesses will approach or even set a new record high set earlier this year.

Mike Mayo, a senior analyst at Wells Fargo, said that the banking industry is currently in a "golden window period" for industry development: the two profit pillars of investment business representing Wall Street and retail credit for the general real economy have achieved simultaneous growth. Leading U.S.

banks rely on corporate capital market financing business to earn generous fees, and last month's unprecedented large-scale SpaceX IPO was a key boost. At the same time, geopolitical turmoil such as the Iran conflict has intensified the price fluctuations of various assets, and risk trading businesses are also seeing market dividends.

Mayo said in an interview: "This quarter saw the birth of the largest IPO in history, and the total volume of M&A transactions is expected to hit a new annual high.

Stock and fixed-income transactions are simultaneously increasing in many places around the world." The release of this quarter's financial reports from major banks coincides with a rare positive cycle for the industry. In the past few years, the banking industry has been under constant pressure.

It has to deal with the high interest rate environment and is worried about the economic recession caused by inflation. Now there are multiple positive resonances: Wall Street's investment and financing business is booming, the quality of residents' credit assets is stable, and corporate loan demand is picking up for the first time in a long time.

Mayo commented: "It is difficult to encounter a more favorable industry environment than now." He added that, coupled with the Trump administration's policy of deregulating the banking industry, the financial sector has outperformed the market for two consecutive years.

This eye-catching performance has also raised market expectations, and investors will pay close attention to whether the industry's high prosperity can continue until 2027.

JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, and Goldman Sachs will release their second-quarter results on Tuesday morning, and Morgan Stanley will disclose its financial results on Wednesday.

Core profit engine: substantial increase in revenue from trading and investment banking business Chris McGraty, an analyst at investment bank KBW, estimates that the overall investment banking revenue of the above-mentioned institutions is expected to surge by 26% year-on-year, and the trading business revenue will increase by 14% year-on-year.

The lead underwriters of SpaceX's IPO are Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley. The two investment banks have booked hundreds of millions of dollars in underwriting fees for this listing alone. In addition, the investment banks also undertook the bond issuance business of this newly listed company to obtain service fees.

At the same time, they have the opportunity to undertake the wealth management business of the company's new billionaires and multi-millionaires. Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley also made a "soft dollar" gain from SpaceX's oversubscribed IPO, said Jay Ritter, emeritus professor of finance at the University of Florida's Warrington College of Business.

SpaceX listing ceremony on the Nasdaq trading floor in New York on June 12, 2026. SpaceX CEO Elon Musk spoke remotely on a large screen from the Starbase headquarters in Texas. Ritter explained that the so-called soft dollars are essentially the sharing fees that hedge funds pay investment banks to obtain oversubscribed IPO shares.

"The biggest profit that the IPO business brings to investment banks is not the basic underwriting fee, but the channel of allocating new shares to hedge funds and active mutual funds in exchange for soft dollar shares." McGraty analyzed that the increase in trading business revenue comes from two major drivers: the stock market continued to rise this

quarter, which led to increased stock trading volume; the conflict in Iran pushed up oil prices, interest rates, and exchange rates, and the trading activity in the fixed income market increased significantly.

"Banks are now able to fully capture the benefits of market fluctuations, whereas in previous cycles they often suffered losses due to misjudgments." Real economy: demand for corporate loans picks up But Mayo believes that the more noteworthy benefits this quarter come from the traditional commercial banking sector.

The commercial loan business, which has been sluggish in the past few years, has reached an inflection point: on the one hand, banks and private credit institutions compete for corporate customers; on the other hand, the artificial intelligence industry has spawned a capital expenditure boom across the entire industry chain, and corporate financing needs have fully recovered.

"Enterprises have regarded market uncertainty as the norm and no longer wait and see. They have built new factories, expanded production lines, and promoted investment in various projects. Loan demand has rebounded across the board," Mayo said. This trend is good for regional banks (e.g.

), the proportion of commercial loans in its business structure is much higher than that of large all-purpose banks such as JPMorgan Chase. On May 6, 2026, the construction site of a $16 billion data center built by Related Digital for Oracle and OpenAI in Selling, Michigan.

A large number of local residents oppose the project, worried about the occupation of farmland, tight water and electricity supply and rising costs. Retail banking also remained solid.

In an environment of low unemployment, the overdue rates of residential mortgage loans, car loans, and credit cards have remained low, and losses on credit assets have been controllable.

Risk worries in the private credit market: Although no new large-scale risk events have occurred recently and market concerns have eased, there is still the possibility of thunderstorms in this field.

After the collapse of subprime auto lender Tricolor last year, JPMorgan CEO Dimon warned investors: "Once you find one cockroach, there is often a whole nest hiding behind it." Competition for deposits intensifies: Many banks have to raise deposit interest rates to retain and attract depositors' funds.

In an environment where interest rates are stabilizing or rising, banks' net interest margins will continue to be squeezed. The financial sector has outperformed the market for two consecutive years.

Instead of simply focusing on the outstanding performance of this quarter, investors are more concerned about whether the current boom cycle with multiple positive benefits can be sustained. McGraty said: "We all know that this quarter's results will be very impressive.

The core question that the market is really concerned about is whether the prosperity can be sustained. Can all these dividends be maintained in the long term?"

“存储双雄”又暴跌！📉韩国央行竟然这么说

一、核心政策表态：韩国央行明确周期未到顶
韩国央行周一发布报告反驳市场 “芯片周期见顶” 悲观预期，核心观点：
当前全球半导体供需格局依旧供不应求，AI 基建持续拉动存储需求，但高端 HBM 产能扩产受工艺、良率约束，供给释放速度远慢于需求；
本轮周期和传统存储复苏完全不同，底层逻辑是 AI 全面渗透带来产业变革，云厂商持续资本开支具备长期持续性；
HBM 等定制化高端存储生产壁垒高、扩产周期长，行业高景气扩张将维持较长时间；
多家海外大行（大摩、小摩、高盛）一致预判，至少 2027 年前半导体需求保持强劲。

二、盘面反差：利好发声当日，存储龙头大幅杀跌
消息落地当天韩股走弱，存储双雄同步暴跌：
SK 海力士大跌近 8%，三星电子跌近 4%；
近期调整幅度显著：7 月 2 日三星大跌 9%、SK 海力士跌近 15%；即便三星公布二季度近 599 亿美元超预期利润，股价依旧高开低走大跌 7%。

三、股价下跌四大核心矛盾
1. 资金提前博弈远期供给过剩（核心分歧）
短期 HBM 订单锁满、现货紧缺是共识，但市场担忧三星、SK 海力士持续加码先进产线，2027-2028 年集中扩产落地后，存储供需反转；周期板块资金习惯提前计价远期利空，高位估值先行消化。

2. 高位获利盘集中兑现
上半年 HBM 行情驱动存储股大幅上涨，7 月进入机构半年净值结算窗口，海外公募、对冲基金集中止盈减仓，抛压集中释放。

3. 基本面结构性割裂，估值存在隐忧
景气高度集中于 AI 服务器 HBM，手机、PC 等消费端存储需求持续疲软；市场担忧 AI 资本开支依赖举债，行业估值高位、一旦云厂商收缩 capex，存储需求弹性快速回落。

4. 中东地缘风险压制风险偏好

“存储双雄”又暴跌！📉韩国央行竟然这么说

一、核心政策表态：韩国央行明确周期未到顶

当前全球半导体供需格局依旧供不应求，AI 基建持续拉动存储需求，但高端 HBM 产能扩产受工艺、良率约束，供给释放速度远慢于需求；
本轮周期和传统存储复苏完全不同，底层逻辑是 AI 全面渗透带来产业变革，云厂商持续资本开支具备长期持续性；
HBM 等定制化高端存储生产壁垒高、扩产周期长，行业高景气扩张将维持较长时间；
多家海外大行（大摩、小摩、高盛）一致预判，至少 2027 年前半导体需求保持强劲。

SK 海力士大跌近 8%，三星电子跌近 4%；

战争在打，能定价的市场却没开门 —— CME 重开前的最后 3 小时（07-12）

⭐ 一句话：过去三个小时中东连续升级，而 $BTC 一动没动 —— 34 美元。今早报 $64,135，现在 $64,169。

期间发生了什么：伊朗革命卫队正式宣布关闭霍尔木兹海峡、伊朗导弹/无人机打了六个国家的美军基地、科威特一座海上石油钻井平台被炸、美军刚刚打掉霍尔木兹周边的反舰导弹与防空系统。

别把这个「没反应」读成「市场认为无所谓」。真相是：能给这些消息定价的市场（原油、黄金、美股期货）现在根本没开门，而 crypto 是 0.35 倍地量的周末薄盘。定价不会不发生，只是被推迟到 —— 今天 15:00（加州时间）CME 重开，距现在不到 3 小时。

🌍 一、宏观：三天的战争，一分钱都还没定价
美股、原油、黄金全部休市，第二天了。屏幕上挂着的还是周五收盘锚：纳指 26,281.61（+0.29%）｜标普 7,575.39（+0.42%）｜道指 52,637.01（+0.29%）｜费半 12,967.16（+0.06%）｜NVDA $210.96（+4.03%）领涨｜VIX 15.03｜WTI 原油 $71.41｜黄金 $4,113.70。

这一整排数字全部是【战前定价】—— 周五收盘时，海峡还没「关」、六国美军基地还没挨打、科威特油井还没被炸。它们已经过期了。今天 15:00 CME 重开 = 油/金/美股期货一次性补定价三天地缘，这是本周第一个、也是最大的跳空窗口。

🛢️ 二、国际形势：「关了」和「没关」同时是真的
升级是真的：美军一周内第三轮空袭伊朗（约 140 个目标），阿巴斯港、格什姆岛均遭袭；伊朗弹道导弹反击卡塔尔、阿曼、巴林、科威特、约旦、阿联酋六国境内美军目标；伊朗波斯湾海峡管理局称海峡「目前无法通行」，革命卫队正式宣布关闭。

但美国中央司令部、特朗普、联合海上信息中心三方同时否认：海峡是国际水道、南部（阿曼一侧）航道双向通行正常、美军护航就位。

谁在说真话？看船，不看嘴 —— 过去 24 小时仅 11 艘商船通过（8 油轮 + 3 货船），正常日均约 34 艘、峰值 59 艘。独立研究机构 HFI Research 最新观察也对得上：伊朗一侧航道仍有船只，但整体航运十分冷清。

👉 结论：这不是「封锁」，是【通行量砍掉三分之二的争议性半关闭】。差别很大 —— 完全封锁油价是跳空级，打折封锁只是溢价级。

❗ 但今天有两处质变：
1）科威特国家石油公司一座海上钻井平台被无人机炸伤 —— 此前挨打的都是船只和军事目标，这是第一个被直接击中的能源生产设施。供给端的性质从「运输受阻」变成了「产能受损」。
2）美军成体系打击了霍尔木兹周边的反舰导弹阵地、防空系统、革命卫队快艇 —— 问题从「伊朗想不想关海峡」变成了「伊朗还有没有能力关」。方向是拧着的：打击进行中 = 短期利多油价；封锁能力被逐步摧毁 = 中期把溢价往回压。这两股力量会在 15:00 CME 开盘时正面打架，别想当然认定只有一个方向。

⚠️ 还有一处要升级理解：伊朗议员今日明说「今后霍尔木兹海峡的管理将由伊朗方面安排，这是伊朗的【最终】政策」。伊朗要的不是一次临时封锁，是改掉海峡的【管理制度】。如果这成真，油价里要长期含一块结构性溢价，而不是打一波就回落的脉冲。

同时谈判在加速：阿曼「双航道」方案（南航道自由通行 / 北航道需伊朗批准、不收费）；伊阿同意继续技术+政治谈判、卡塔尔参与；伊拉克总理周一访华盛顿签油气协议 + 重建绕开霍尔木兹的输油管（需 2-3 年）。

📊 三、技术面（强弱：ETH ＞ BTC ＞ SOL）
🔵 BTC $64,169（24h -0.17%）｜区间 63,605–64,439
　1H 站上 MA20/MA50（64,010 / 64,081），1H MACD 柱转正（+47）= 极微弱的企稳
　但 4H MACD 柱仍为负（-64），动能没回来；日线 RSI 53.5、MACD 零轴下方金叉
　🔴 分水岭：日线 MA50 = 64,670（昨晚 64,944、前天 65,136 —— 均线在往下走，压顶线自己在下移来找价格）

🟢 ETH $1,820（-0.16%，仍最强）｜区间 1,778–1,829.5
　稳稳站在日线 MA50（1,758）上方｜日线 RSI 58.6、MACD 柱 +43｜4H/1H 全在均线上方

🔴 SOL $77.53（-0.64%，最弱）｜区间 75.58–78.30
　4H 跌破 MA20（77.85）和 MA50（79.25）= 空头排列，RSI 45.5

⭐ 成交量才是今天最要命的一格：BTC 最近一根完整日线成交量只有 20 日均量的 0.35 倍；盘中 1H 量比 BTC 0.45、ETH 0.59、SOL 0.42 —— 地量在加深，不是在恢复。
🧭 换句话说：不是「BTC 冷静地消化了战争」，是【房间里根本没人】。周末薄盘里的价格不是信息，这三个小时的「稳」不能当成「市场消化了战争」来读。

⚙️ 四、衍生品结构
资金费率（8小时）：BTC +0.0088% / +0.0061%（温和偏多）｜ETH 【-0.0037%】（比今早更负）/ +0.0020%｜SOL +0.0050%
未平仓合约（OI）24h 几乎没动：BTC $471.5 亿（+0.19%）｜ETH $251.9 亿（+0.03%）｜SOL $50.5 亿（-0.62%，唯一减仓）
24h 爆仓：BTC $1,780 万（多头 1.89 : 1 被打）｜ETH $3,434 万（多 1.18 : 1）｜SOL $1,105 万（多头 10 : 1 被血洗）

⭐ 本段最重要的变化：ETH 那台「轧空发动机」的燃料基本烧完了 —— 前天空头还被轧 4.8:1，今天已经反过来是多头在挨打。ETH 还是最强的，但托着它上涨的强制平仓买盘正在消失。SOL 是反面：多头在被单边收割。

🎯 五、BTC 核心指标
🔴 现货溢价：-$41.4 / -0.0645%（今早 -$39.5）—— 不但没转正，还略微走阔。美国机构现货买盘持续缺席，这条线一天不转正，反弹就一天缺主力。
🔴 恐惧贪婪指数：26（恐惧），与今早持平
🔵 BTC DVOL（隐含波动率）36.44 ｜ ETH DVOL 51.08
⭐ 隐含 vs 已实现（纠正一个想当然）：BTC 隐含 36.3 vs 【7 日已实现 34.9】= 只贵了 1.4 个点，波动率并不便宜；只有跟 30 日已实现 42.1 比才显得低。
　👉 所以别用「波动率白菜价」当买期权的理由。真正的理由是【窗口里塞着两颗有日期的炸弹】（今天 CME 重开、周二 CPI），而隐含波动率是按过去两周的平静定价的。
🔵 MaxPain：7/13 = 63,500（PCR 2.03，put 明显偏重）｜7/14 = 63,500｜7/15-16 = 64,000

🧭 六、综合判断 + 交易建议
核心矛盾：一场正在打的战争 vs 一组还没定价的市场 —— 而 crypto 在一个空房间里独自报价。
⏳ 核心纪律：距 15:00 CME 重开不到 3 小时。【地量 + 一个已知的跳空窗口 = 现在开单边是赌头条，不是交易】。想表达波动就用跨式（7/15–7/17 到期），不赌方向，也不怕「打谈交替」来回反转。

👉 BTC：分水岭仍是日线 MA50 【64,670】
　· 破 63,600 才做空（止损 64,700，目标 62,400 → 61,500）
　· 放量站上 64,670 且现货溢价转正 → 空头框架作废，看 68,000–70,000；溢价不转正就当假突破
　· 没破位不空，没站上不追。中间这段就是不该有仓位的地方。

👉 ETH：最强、站稳 1,758 偏多，但轧空燃料烧完 + 费率转更负，别追高。回踩 1,780–1,800 才是轻多的位置，目标 1,860。

👉 SOL：最弱，4H 已转空排，多头被单边割 10:1 —— 想做多别选它。

⚠️ 方向别想当然：新闻显示这是【半关闭 + 谈判在加速】，不是全面封锁 → 油的跳空幅度大概率小于「海峡关闭」这个标题所暗示的。而且上一轮升级（7/9）黄金是【跌】的（油涨→通胀→加息重定价→压金价）。别指望「数字黄金」叙事这次会救 BTC。

📅 风险事件
· 今天 15:00（加州）—— CME Globex 重开，油/金/美股期货补定价三天地缘，本周最大跳空窗口
· 地缘尾部：「打谈交替」是固定节奏（打→谈→打→缓和），若周内传出「伊朗同意重开海峡」，追空的会被反向绞杀
· 周一 —— OPEC 月度原油报告｜美联储沃勒讲话｜伊拉克总理访华盛顿签油气协议

· 周三 —— 中国二季度 GDP（预期 4.5%）｜美国 6 月 PPI｜韩国央行（预期加息至 2.75%）｜周四 美国零售销售

Two things can be true at the same time, and right now both of them are screaming at us.

Wall Street keeps telling you the oil market is structurally oversupplied, Brent’s sliding toward $60–65 by year-end, gasoline’s going back under $3.80, and inventories are magically going to rebuild by Q4. That story has charts. It has consensus. It has every big bank nodding along.

And it’s not wrong.

But here’s the part they don’t put in the headline: we’ve already spent the emergency fund.

The Strategic Petroleum Reserve is at its lowest level since April 1983 a 43-year low. Total U.S. crude inventories, commercial plus strategic, are sitting at the lowest point since 1984. Distillate diesel, the stuff that moves the trucks and the trains and the farm equipment is running as much as 12% below seasonal norms. And on top of all that, one of the world’s largest diesel exporters just hit the brakes and pulled barrels off the market through the end of July.

That’s not a theory. 

That’s what the EIA numbers actually say.

We used the buffers to manage today’s shock. Now the buffers are thin. And the next shock doesn’t need to be another war in the Strait of Hormuz. 

A bad hurricane season in the Gulf, one refinery going down at the wrong time, or Russia deciding the ban feels good and extends it any one of those and the math gets ugly fast. 

Because there’s less cushion than there was six months ago.

But that’s only half the story.

The grid side isn’t even pretending to be temporary.

We’ve got more generation and storage sitting in interconnection queues than the entire installed capacity of the United States. 

Transformer lead times are stretching to four years. 

Electricity prices are already rising nearly twice as fast as general inflation. 

And none of this is because of a temporary war or a temporary weather event. This is what happens when you take a grid built for a decade of flat demand and suddenly slam it with AI data centers that want to add load faster than we can physically build the hardware to serve it.

The mismatch is structural. 

The analysts who actually model this stuff don’t expect it to ease before 2028 at the earliest.

So here’s where we are. 
We burned through the oil buffers to handle one crisis. We’re running out of time and transformers to handle the next one. And the people telling you everything’s fine, just look at production are measuring the wrong thing.

Production tells you what we can make.

Inventories, lead times, and queues tell you whether we can actually deliver it when something goes sideways.

The next 90 days are going to decide which version of the story is true.

Watch July 31 does that Russian diesel ban actually end, or does it become the new normal?

Watch hurricane season because the Gulf is already running on thin distillate margins. Watch whether anybody actually starts refilling the SPR instead of just talking about it.
Because if we take another hit before those buffers start rebuilding, the structurally oversupplied, everything’s fine narrative is going to age like milk.

Two things can be true at once.
We’re not out of energy. But we’re running out of the slack that makes the system work when the unexpected shows up. And the unexpected has a habit of showing up exactly when you’ve already spent the rainy-day fund.

Nigeria Exceeds OPEC Crude Oil Production Quota, Records Highest Oil Output in Over Six Years

Nigeria has surpassed its OPEC crude oil production quota, recording an average crude oil output of 1.56 million barrels per day (bpd) in June 2026, approximately 4 percent above the approved quota of 1.5 million bpd.

Including condensates, the country’s total oil production rose to 1.735 million bpd, the highest level recorded in over six years and the fourth consecutive month of production growth.

The improved performance has been attributed to stable production across key oil fields and the absence of major pipeline disruptions, reflecting sustained efforts to strengthen operational efficiency and enhance security within the oil and gas sector.

The milestone represents a significant turnaround for Nigeria’s petroleum industry following years of production challenges caused by oil theft, pipeline vandalism, and underinvestment. It also reinforces growing confidence in ongoing reforms aimed at increasing output, boosting government revenue, and supporting economic growth.
